macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah: Land Sale with Measurement and the Problem of Ona'ah and Bitul Mekach

micro_segments.title: Overview of the Mishnah: Beit Kor sale with precise vs. approximate measurement
Why Ona'ah does not apply to land sales and how Bitul Mekach is avoided
Tosafot's distinction between measured land with deception and unmeasured land with known imprecision

macro_segments.title: The Case of Unstated Measurement: Whether Stam (unmodified) Land Sale Implies Precision or Approximate Tolerance

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's question: what is the halacha when neither 'precise' nor 'more or less' is stated?
Proof from the Mishna that stam is like 'more or less' allowing 1/24 tolerance
The Gemara's rejection of alternative readings and conclusion that stam allows tolerance
Tosafot's clarification: tolerance only applies when the specific field is known and unmeasured

macro_segments.title: The Seller's Prerogative: When Overage Goes to Buyer and When Seller May Demand Money or Land Back

micro_segments.title: Distinction between buyer receiving too little (discount) and too much (seller's choice)
The tension between the Mishna (seller's prerogative) and the Baraita (forcing sale/purchase)
Reconciliation: force the seller to sell only applies when land prices have fallen; seller must sell at current rate

macro_segments.title: The Limits of Seller's Prerogative: Absolute vs. Proportional Amounts and When Land Becomes Usable

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna's view: seller loses prerogative once absolute overage amount is usable
Rav Nachman's view: prerogative limits scale proportionally per kor or seah sold
Rava's challenge to Rav Nachman using the Mishna's absolute amounts (nine kav, half kav)
Gemara's readings of Mishna cases: each applies only to its stated size, not proportionally scaled

macro_segments.title: Unresolved Issues: Field-to-Garden Conversion and Contiguous Property Exceptions

micro_segments.title: Rav Ashi's question: if overage becomes usable as a garden before measurement, does seller lose prerogative?
Principle: if overage is contiguous to seller's own field, buyer cannot force him to buy
Further unresolved questions about physical obstacles (wells, canals, paths) affecting contiguity

macro_segments.title: The Extent of Return: When Overage Exceeds Tolerance, Entire Excess Amount Is Returned

micro_segments.title: Mishna language clarified: not just the excess over tolerance, but entire overage is returned

macro_segments.title: Contradictory Language in Sale Agreement: Which Clause Controls When Precision and Approximate Language Conflict

micro_segments.title: Ben Nanas' principle: in contradictory language, the second (later) clause voids the first

topical_tags.term: Mida Bechevel (measured by the yardstick / precise measurement)
Tosafot distinction: measured with deception vs. unmeasured with implicit imprecision
Hen chaser hen yeter (more or less)
Rav Huna vs. Rav Nachman debate on proportional scaling
Rava's challenge using absolute amounts from Mishna
Nine kav threshold for usable field overage
Half kav threshold for usable garden overage
Rova kav (1/4 kav) according to Rabbi Akiva
Samuch lesadehu (contiguous to seller's own field)
Ben Nanas: second clause nullifies first in contradictory language
